root_text.sn15.12:1.3: yaṁ, bhikkhave, passeyyātha sukhitaṁ susajjitaṁ, niṭṭhamettha gantabbaṁ:

translation_text.sn15.12:1.2: “Mendicants, this transmigration has no known beginning. …
translation_text.sn15.12:1.3: When you see someone in a good way, in a happy state, you should conclude:
translation_text.sn15.12:1.4: ‘In all this long time, we too have undergone the same thing.’
translation_text.sn15.12:1.6: This transmigration has no known beginning. …
translation_text.sn15.12:1.8: This is quite enough for you to become disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding all conditions.”

reference_text.sn15.12:1.1: csp1ed12.394, csp2ed12.394, ms12S2_866, msdiv135, ndp13.158, pts-vp-pli2.187
